public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as quiet-room mediator.
appearance: Sel Vale carries the marks of quiet-room mediator in ink, rain, and polished cloth: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Moves through conversation like someone who solves visible problems first and interprets meaning second. In Transpolity, that reads as someone boundary-crossing and adaptive, speaking to whichever audience or protocol is presently in front of them, and the habits of a quiet-room mediator still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of quiet-room mediator in and around Aurora Light Cathedral, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Aurora Light Cathedral while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Getting involved with Sel Vale can expose the party to the everyday consequences of pressure that others only discuss in the abstract.
Sel Vale can offer grounded local knowledge once the party proves it is useful rather than merely curious.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained.
public_signal: Sel Vale carries the look of a practiced local operator whose authority is read through place, familiarity, and the reactions of others more than formal insignia.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Aurora Light Cathedral: the part of Aurora Light Cathedral where their work actually matters day to day
who_avoids_them: those who only appear when things break

first_impression: Sel Vale presents as Moves through conversation like someone who solves visible problems first and interprets meaning second. In Transpolity, that reads as someone boundary-crossing and adaptive, speaking to whichever audience or protocol is presently in front of them, and the habits of a quiet-room mediator still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Quiet-Room Mediator, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Transpolity.
what_they_refuse: Sel Vale refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Quiet-Room Mediator too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Sel Vale can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Sel Vale adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Sel Vale is Known locally through day-to-day work as quiet-room mediator. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Quiet-Room Mediator.
social_texture: Sel Vale speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Aurora Light Cathedral, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Sel touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Sel Vale when the party needs a socially grounded face for Aurora Light Cathedral: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
